mechanism_narrative: Increases in the federal funds rate raise corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, directly compressing valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns are positive in >65% of rate increase periods, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate equity sell-offs, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.4pp in a single month
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered months, or signal fails to separate bull/bear periods with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous quarterly growth in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms broad-based consumer demand expansion, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth periods yield negative 60d returns in >55% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp monthly declines in the industrial production index indicate immediate supply-side contraction that forces firms to cut capital expenditures, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5d forward returns are positive in >70% of industrial production decline months, or signal loses predictive power during recovery phases (Rank IC < 0.01).

mechanism_narrative: Monthly CPIAUCSL growth exceeding 3.0% signals accelerating inflation that forces central banks to tighten policy, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d returns are positive in >65% of high-inflation months, or threshold model fails to outperform buy-and-hold with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly increases in total nonfarm payrolls reflect expanding economic capacity and rising household income, which directly supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Payroll growth periods produce negative 60d returns in >60% of observations, or rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining housing starts and rising retail sales indicates shifting consumer spending away from durable goods, which signals near-term demand fragility and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Divergence periods yield positive 5d returns in >65% of cases, or interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during a monetary easing cycle amplifies liquidity-driven equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Signal generates negative 20d returns in >60% of easing cycle observations, or regime-conditional outperformance disappears in walk-forward tests.
